title: What was NERVs purpose for Asuka
display_title: What was NERVs purpose for Asuka
wikitext:
[]: What was NERV's purpose for Asuka?             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Thu Feb 21, 2013 1:36 pm Everything within the world of NGE has a perfect mother child balance. If you consider the first 6 episodes, the arrival of Shinji seems to fit perfectly with the kind of fantasy world (kept in a kind of perfect bubble) that Gendo would desire. He has his son fighting in Unit 1 which has Yui's soul inside alongside a the physical clone of Yui which has the soul of the mother of humanity(Lilith). With these kind of dynamics in place, why is Asuka's presence necessary for NERV? I get that her mom's soul fragment is in Unit 2, but why didn't they just scrap it and forget all about Asuka since it doesn't play in real part in 3rd impact? It just seems like Asuka's presence is a completely alien one within the perfect haven that had already been designed. Any thoughts in this matter?

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Feb 21, 2013 2:22 pm They were hedging their bets. If Shinji didn't work out, for whatever reason, they had Asuka ready to go. If Asuka and Shinji didn't work out, they were preparing Toji. Etc. There was also a very real risk that Shinji would be KIA before he could be used to initiate Third Impact. I think it's also important to distinguish just which "they" we're talking about here. Shinji seems to have been specifically more important to Gendo than to Seele, for instance. Both parties would have been just fine with a Shinji-mediated 3I, but Gendo probably wouldn't have been fine with a non-Shinji-mediated 3I. Shinji was critical to Gendo's plan, but interchangeable in Seele's plan.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Thu Feb 21, 2013 2:40 pm They need her to kill angels. Three Evas are better than two to ensure success. 'Tis the same reason they wanted Toji and Unit-03.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Feb 21, 2013 2:51 pm Asuka is just a disposable weapons system in the larger scheme of things. She also checks the boxes for hot-blooded pilot and brash interloper who disrupts the OTP for the middle of the series, just as you'd expect in a super-robot show.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Feb 23, 2013 6:34 pm More pilots, more bang for your buck. Tines is right, though. She's pretty much a use-and-toss kind of deal.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat Feb 23, 2013 6:45 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: She's pretty much a use-and-toss kind of deal.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Feb 23, 2013 6:54 pm Yep, Asuka is NERV's equivalent of a 'red shirt' to throw at any problems that they can't risk something important on, like Rei or Shogouki. You know, missions like suicide volcano diving. Acceptable losses to guard the Adam sample. A decoy for both Arael and Armisael. They probably wanted to use her as Lilliel bait too before Shinji fucked it all up.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sat Feb 23, 2013 8:35 pm EvangelionFan wrote: pffffff hahahahahahaha

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Feb 24, 2013 8:45 am NemZ wrote: Yep, Asuka is NERV's equivalent of a 'red shirt' to throw at any problems that they can't risk something important on, like Rei or Shogouki. You know, missions like suicide volcano diving. Acceptable losses to guard the Adam sample. A decoy for both Arael and Armisael. They probably wanted to use her as Lilliel bait too before Shinji fucked it all up.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sun Feb 24, 2013 9:22 am NemZ wrote: Yep, Asuka is NERV's equivalent of a 'red shirt' to throw at any problems that they can't risk something important on, like Rei or Shogouki. You know, missions like suicide volcano diving. Acceptable losses to guard the Adam sample. A decoy for both Arael and Armisael. They probably wanted to use her as Lilliel bait too before Shinji fucked it all up.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Feb 24, 2013 9:43 am Mr. Tines wrote: She also checks the boxes for hot-blooded pilot and brash interloper who disrupts the OTP for the middle of the series, just as you'd expect in a super-robot show.

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Sun Feb 24, 2013 4:36 pm Well, Yui's complete soul was transferred to 01, but only part of Kyoko's soul was in 02 (until some point later?). How does that part play into Seele, Gendo's or Nerv's plans? I think only having part of Kyoko in there would be better for Seele because then they would only have to worry about manipulating Asuka into initiating 3I/HIP instead of having to consider what Kyoko would do.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Feb 24, 2013 7:33 pm user-02 wrote: There's no doubt that Shinji and Rei are vitally more important to Nerv's grand plan.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sun Feb 24, 2013 10:04 pm NemZ wrote: I said Shogouki (unit 01), not Shinji. Gendo really would probably have preferred he was never involved at all. He was gambling on Rei and the dummy system to handle it all, with Asuka as side option.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 25, 2013 7:22 am ...you mean when they put it on lockdown and didn't want him doing anything? At that point Gendo clearly still would prefer Shinji just stay out of the way, though close enough they could use him in a pinch. If Asuka was able to pilot he'd have never been allowed to sortie vs. Armisarael either.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Feb 25, 2013 7:38 am NemZ wrote: Gendo really would probably have preferred he was never involved at all.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 25, 2013 8:50 am Because Asuka wasn't ready and Rei was unexpectedly incapacitated. If he actually planned to have Shinji involved then it makes no sense at all that he wasn't trained from childhood for the job, including bringing him in on the big secrets and using his mother as fuel to make him a zealot for the cause.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Mon Feb 25, 2013 10:10 am NemZ wrote: Because Asuka wasn't ready and Rei was unexpectedly incapacitated. If he actually planned to have Shinji involved then it makes no sense at all that he wasn't trained from childhood for the job, including bringing him in on the big secrets and using his mother as fuel to make him a zealot for the cause.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Feb 25, 2013 10:20 am user-02 wrote: A man of Gendo's resources, ambitions, and capabilities would have found an alternative solution to calling Shinji up to Nerv in the first place if he didn't want to make use of Shinji. He knew all too well what a basket case of nerves and neuroses Shinji was, and he wouldn't have bought the cow if he didn't think the milk was worth it. (Case in point: he could have plucked anybody from that school, i.e., Toji, at a moment's notice if he wanted to. He picked Shinji for a reason, and the reason was that Unit-01 required Shinji).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 25, 2013 10:57 am ^ Yeah pretty much, but if he had planned to use Shinji what I said certainly would have happened and the series would have just been typical hotblooded mecha stuff, not a psychiatric puppet show... unless Asuka became the new main character.
